steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between two independent clauses: “plastic-bag consumption decreased by up to ninety [BLANK]” and “Such taxes are subject to what economists call the ‘rebound effect’.” The skill tested is boundary punctuation between sentences.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘percent, such’: places only a comma between the two independent clauses, creating a comma splice. A comma alone cannot join two main clauses without a coordinating conjunction. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘percent and such’: uses the coordinating conjunction “and” but omits the required comma before it. A coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses must be preceded by a comma. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘percent. Such’: uses a period to correctly end the first sentence and begin a new sentence with “Such taxes.” A period is a valid boundary between two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘percent such’: places no punctuation or conjunction between the two independent clauses, creating a run-on sentence. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
